steps.title: Identify the old barrier
steps.content: Before copyright ended, adapting Sherlock Holmes required paying a fee and gaining approval from strict copyright holders. Those requirements made adaptations costly and difficult.

steps.title: Infer the effect
steps.content: A claims the original stories will become harder to find, which the text does not suggest. B shifts to public interest in detective stories. D concerns returning past fees. Removing fees and approval barriers makes new adaptations easier and cheaper. The answer is C.
